name: INFLAMMATORY MYOFIBROBLASTIC TUMOR
definition: PLASMA CELL GRANULOMA = INFLAMMATORY PSEUDOTUMOR = INFLAMMATORY FIBROSARCOMA XANTHOMATOUS PSEUDOTUMOR = HISTIOCYTOMA = XANTHOGRANULOMA = FIBROXANTHOMA PSEUDOLYMPHOMA rare neoplasm of intermediate biologic behavior with a low rate (< 5%) of metastases based on

demographics.Genetics: rearrangement in anaplastic lymphoma kinase (ALK) gene = tyrosine receptor kinase
demographics.Age: any; young adult and child; M < F
demographics.Histo: myofibroblastic spindle cell proliferation in a loosely myxoid to dense collagenous

discriminating_features: nonspecific varied imaging finding depending on site of origin and composition of
hypo- / isoattenuating compared with skeletal muscle depending on amount of myxoid /
(rarely) aggressive intramural masses ± invasion through gastric wall into peritoneal cavity

general: nonspecific varied imaging finding depending on site of origin and composition of
US: ill- / well-defined heterogeneous hypo- / hyperechoic solid mass of mixed echotexture
± shadowing echogenic calcifications / central hypoechoic regions
prominent vascularity with low-resistance arterial waveform on color Doppler
CT: hypo- / isoattenuating compared with skeletal muscle depending on amount of myxoid /
(rarely) aggressive intramural masses ± invasion through gastric wall into peritoneal cavity
MR: typically T1-hypointense relative to skeletal muscle and hyperintense on T2WI
densely fibrotic tumor may be dark on both sequences
useful in detecting primary tumor, local recurrence, distant metastases
mesentery / omentum: bulky well-demarcated homo- / heterogeneous spherical / multilobulated mass

clinical.Rx: complete surgical resection → propensity for local recurrence
